name: Bob Haggart
description: American double bass player, composer, and arranger (1914–1998)
abstract: Robert Sherwood Haggart (March 13, 1914 – December 2, 1998) was an American Dixieland jazz double bass player, composer, and arranger. Although he is associated with Dixieland, he was one of the finest rhythm bassists of the Swing Era.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: To have been able to record a record with what essentially was Soprano Summit. Davern, Wilber, Haggart on bass, Dave Frishberg on piano and Bucky, and me on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Sutton
about_quotes.evidence: I’ll never forget when I was with Bob Haggart and Yank Lawson, and it was with a band, it was called the World’s Greatest Jazz Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Bob Haggart and I had done so many things that Bob Crosby got credit for, that we want to get together and organize a group.

about_quotes.speaker: Nicki Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: I listen to Milt Hinton a lot and Bobby Haggart, I got to meet him a couple of times.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nicki Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe. Utica, New York, November 6, 2010 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I brought men like... Bobby Haggart of course of fame... And it was their organization. Nobody talked. If you talked to any of the guys they’ll say, “I didn’t talk, I just looked.”... Haggart, when Haggart said, “I’ve got to get out of here”
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: although the World's Greatest Jazz Band was a great band because Bobby Haggart was a beautiful musician and arranger, he wrote for it... I used such names as Ray McKinley drums, Bob Haggart bass.
